did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper utilized 'machine learning' (ML) algorithms integrated into a 3-dimensional total body photography (3D-TBP) and digital dermoscopy (DD) system. Specifically, the device provided automatic lesion segmentation, automatic change detection in maps, and calculated individual malignancy risk scores for melanoma detection in high-risk patients.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents original experimental data from a retrospective study involving 1,053 high-risk patients. It reports specific results including the number of 3D-TBP maps generated, lesion counts, histological diagnoses of excised lesions, and machine learning algorithm outcomes (e.g., malignancy scores, change detection rates), which characterizes it as an original research study rather than a review.

title: Use of 3-dimensional total body photography and digital dermoscopy with artificial intelligence algorithms for early melanoma detection in high-risk patients
brief_summary: This retrospective study evaluated the use of 3-dimensional total body photography (3D-TBP) and digital dermoscopy (DD) with integrated machine learning algorithms for early melanoma detection in 1,053 high-risk patients. The system generated 2,929 maps and provided automatic lesion segmentation, change detection, and malignancy risk scores. Among 337 excised lesions, 47 were melanomas, with 78.7% of these showing automatically detected changes. The mean malignancy score was 5.3 for melanomas compared to 2.11 for dysplastic nevi. The results indicate that 3D-TBP linked with ML algorithms is useful for follow-up and early detection in high-risk patients.

lesion_types_studied.details: 47 lesions diagnosed as melanomas among 337 excised lesions; 28 in situ and 19 invasive (median Breslow index 0.4 mm). Mean malignancy score 5.3.
64 lesions diagnosed as dysplastic nevi among 337 excised lesions. Mean malignancy score 2.11.
16 lesions diagnosed as common nevi among 337 excised lesions.
41 lesions diagnosed as basal cell carcinomas among 337 excised lesions.
8 lesions diagnosed as squamous cell carcinomas among 337 excised lesions.
8 lesions diagnosed as solar lentigos among 337 excised lesions.
22 lesions with unspecified diagnoses among 337 excised lesions.

all_task_types.details: The system performed 'automatic lesion segmentation' and 'automatically segmented lesions', with a mean of 816.8 segmented lesions per map.
The system performed 'automatic change detection in maps', identifying 78.7% of melanomas as having automatically detected changes.
The system calculated 'individual malignancy risk scores' (DD score on a scale from 0 to 10), with a mean score of 5.3 for melanomas and 2.11 for dysplastic nevi.

datasets_used.name: Hospital Clínic de Barcelona High-Risk Patient Cohort
datasets_used.description: A retrospective cohort of 1,053 high-risk melanoma patients from a tertiary center (Hospital Clínic de Barcelona, Spain) who underwent 3-dimensional total body photography (3D-TBP) and digital dermoscopy (DD) between July 2021 and September 2023.

use.testing: The dataset was used for descriptive analysis of clinical, phenotypical, imaging, and histological characteristics of excised lesions and machine learning algorithm outcomes (malignancy scores, change detection). It served as the source for validating the clinical utility of the integrated ML algorithms in a real-world high-risk setting.
use.other: The study describes the experience with 3D-TBP and DD for early melanoma detection and ML algorithm outcomes in high-risk patients.

metrics_used_to_evaluate_results: Mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map
Percentage of melanomas with automatically detected changes
Percentage of melanomas without detected changes
Percentage of melanomas with no information available

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To analyze clinical, phenotypical, imaging and histological characteristics of excised lesions and ML results.

statistical_methods_used.method: Comparison of means with significance testing
statistical_methods_used.purpose: To compare the mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map between patients with excision and patients with no excisions.

hypothesis_testing.test: Unspecified statistical test (likely t-test or Mann-Whitney U test based on mean comparison and p-value reporting)
hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: There is no difference in the mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map between patients with excision and patients with no excisions.
hypothesis_testing.alternative_hypothesis: There is a difference in the mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map between patients with excision and patients with no excisions.
hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map was significantly higher in patients with excision (1195.6) compared to patients with no excisions (683.5), with a p-value of less than 0.005.

Hospital Clínic de Barcelona High-Risk P.dataset_description: Retrospective cohort of 1,053 high-risk melanoma patients from a tertiary center (Hospital Clínic de Barcelona, Spain) who underwent 3-dimensional total body photography (3D-TBP) and digital dermoscopy (DD) between July 2021 and September 2023.
Hospital Clínic de Barcelona High-Risk P.notes: The study describes the experience with 3D-TBP and DD for early melanoma detection in high-risk patients. The system provided automatic lesion segmentation, automatic change detection in maps, and individual malignancy risk scores. The mean number of automatically segmented lesions per map was significantly higher in patients with excision compared to patients with no excisions (p < 0.005).

architecture_details.description: The paper reports the use of 'research-oriented machine learning (ML) algorithms' integrated into a 3-dimensional total body photography (3D-TBP) and digital dermoscopy (DD) system (specifically the Intelligent Total Body Scanner for Early Detection of Melanoma, iToBoS). The system provides automatic lesion segmentation, automatic change detection in maps, and individual malignancy risk scores. Specific model architecture details (e.g., backbone, layers, heads) are not reported.
architecture_details.system_name: Intelligent Total Body Scanner for Early Detection of Melanoma (iToBoS)
capabilities: Individual malignancy risk scores (DD score on a scale from 0 to 10)

classification_or_output_head.details: The system calculates an individual malignancy risk score. Mean score was 5.3 for melanomas and 2.11 for dysplastic nevi.

machine_learning_or_deep_learning_models: research-oriented machine learning (ML) algorithms integrated into the Intelligent Total Body Scanner for Early Detection of Melanoma (iToBoS) system
preprocessing_techniques: Automatic lesion segmentation (performed by the integrated machine learning algorithms in the 3D-TBP and DD system)
Automatic change detection in maps (performed by the integrated machine learning algorithms)
conclusions: 3D-TBP is useful for follow-up and early detection of melanoma in high-risk patients.
3D-TBP can be linked to ML algorithms with different applications.
These ML algorithms must be validated in clinical trials.
The integration of 3D-TBP with ML algorithms may provide clinically useful information.
important_remarks: The study is a single-center retrospective analysis from a tertiary hospital (Hospital Clínic de Barcelona), which limits generalizability and introduces potential selection bias for high-risk patients.
The paper explicitly states that the machine learning algorithms used must be validated in clinical trials, indicating a lack of external validation in this report.
The dataset is imbalanced with a low number of positive melanoma cases (47 out of 337 excised lesions), which may affect the robustness of the malignancy score evaluation.

The system is a proprietary device (iToBoS) with 'research-oriented' algorithms; specific model architecture details, training data, and hyperparameters are not disclosed, preventing independent verification of the AI performance.
The study reports descriptive statistics and mean comparisons but does not provide standard diagnostic performance metrics such as sensitivity, specificity, or AUC for the ML algorithms.

There is a notable discrepancy in the reported melanoma change detection: the summary states 78.7% showed changes, while the detailed breakdown lists 37 cases (78.7%) with changes, 1 case (0.02%) without, and 9 cases with no information available, totaling 47 melanomas.
